title: cicuta maculata var. victorinii
intro: Cicuta maculata var. victorinii, commonly known as the eastern water hemlock, is a highly toxic and poisonous plant native to eastern North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to act as a muscle relaxant and sedative, and has been used to treat conditions such as insomnia and anxiety. However, caution is advised due to its toxicity, as even small amounts can be fatal. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used in small quantities and under the guidance of a medical professional, often in the form of a tincture or infusion.
